blurb: Supports Alberta's agriculture and food sectors through risk management, lending, food safety, animal health, pest and weed management, irrigation, and market development. Works with producers, processors, and rural communities to grow a sustainable, competitive agri-food industry.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Producers are financially resilient and manage risk effectively
goals.description: Farmers, ranchers, and agribusinesses can access insurance, income stabilization, and financing to manage risk and invest in their operations.

foundational_services.name: Design and administer agricultural risk management programs
foundational_services.desc: Develop and oversee crop insurance, livestock insurance, income stabilization, and lending programs that help producers manage financial risk. Delivered primarily through Agriculture Financial Services Corporation (AFSC).

main_services.name: Protect crops and livestock with insurance

supporting.name: Apply for annual crop insurance
supporting.desc: Protects against crop production losses caused by natural hazards including drought, flooding, hail, frost, excessive moisture, and wildlife damage. Covers annual crops, perennial crops, and honey production.

economic_impact.note: Crop insurance prevents farm bankruptcies and stabilizes rural economies during adverse growing conditions

supporting.name: Apply for livestock price insurance
supporting.desc: Protects cattle and hog producers against drops in market prices. Producers choose a coverage price level and receive an indemnity if the market settlement price falls below it.

economic_impact.note: Price insurance lets producers plan ahead and invest with confidence despite commodity volatility

supporting.name: Apply for hail insurance
supporting.desc: Straight hail insurance provides coverage for crop losses specifically caused by hail damage. Available as a standalone policy or in combination with multi-peril crop insurance.

economic_impact.note: Hail is the single most frequent cause of crop loss in Alberta

supporting.name: File a wildlife damage compensation claim
supporting.desc: Compensation for crop or livestock losses caused by wildlife including waterfowl, elk, deer, bears, and cougars. Administered by AFSC under the wildlife damage compensation program.

economic_impact.note: Compensates producers for losses they cannot reasonably prevent

supporting.name: Enrol in income stabilization support
supporting.desc: Federal-provincial program that provides support when a producer's net income falls significantly below their historical average. Covers income declines from production loss, increased costs, and market conditions.

economic_impact.note: Whole-farm income stabilization prevents cascading financial failure in the agriculture sector

supporting.name: Open a government-matched farm savings account
supporting.desc: Government-matched savings account that helps producers set money aside for small income declines or make investments to mitigate risk. The federal government matches annual deposits up to a specified limit.

economic_impact.note: Matched savings grow producer equity and support on-farm investment

supporting.name: Apply for disaster recovery assistance
supporting.desc: AgriRecovery provides targeted assistance for extraordinary costs caused by natural disasters such as drought, flooding, or disease outbreaks that go beyond existing programs. Triggered by federal-provincial assessment.

economic_impact.note: Disaster-specific support helps producers recover and maintain food production capacity

main_services.name: Get financing for farm and agribusiness operations

supporting.name: Apply for a beginning farmer loan
supporting.desc: Specialized loan products for new and young farmers getting started in agriculture, including the Next Generation Loan and Developing Producer Loan. Lower down payments and flexible terms.
supporting.users: New farmers, young farmers entering agriculture

economic_impact.note: Enabling farm succession supports long-term sector viability and rural employment

supporting.name: Apply for a farm or agribusiness loan
supporting.desc: Loans for established agricultural producers and agribusinesses for land purchases, equipment, operating capital, and value-added processing. Includes the Alberta Producer Loan and Revolving Loan products.

economic_impact.note: Farm lending supports agricultural capital investment and sector growth

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Agriculture is safe, healthy, and well-managed
goals.description: Livestock are healthy and traceable, food is safe, and pests, diseases, and weeds are effectively controlled to protect producers, consumers, and the environment.

foundational_services.name: Regulate animal health and food safety across the supply chain
foundational_services.desc: The Office of the Chief Provincial Veterinarian monitors and responds to animal diseases, oversees meat and dairy inspection, and coordinates with federal agencies on food safety. Agricultural Service Boards deliver local pest and weed control.
foundational_services.users: All Albertans (consumer protection), livestock operators, food processors

supporting.name: Report a suspected animal disease
supporting.desc: Producers and veterinarians must report certain animal diseases within 24 hours. The Chief Provincial Veterinarian coordinates response including quarantine, testing, and control measures to prevent spread.

economic_impact.note: Early disease detection prevents outbreaks that could devastate the livestock sector and close export markets

supporting.name: Register your premises for livestock traceability
supporting.desc: Premises identification is required for all locations where livestock are kept. The system enables rapid response to disease outbreaks by tracking where animals are located.

economic_impact.note: Traceability protects market access — an outbreak without traceability could close billion-dollar export markets

supporting.name: Get guidance on livestock management practices
supporting.desc: Information on beef production, animal nutrition, handling, welfare, forages and pasture management, and mortality management. Includes publications and extension services delivered through AFSC and regional offices.

economic_impact.note: Better livestock management improves animal welfare and production efficiency

main_services.name: Ensure food is safe across the supply chain

supporting.name: Get food safety training and certification
supporting.desc: Training in Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) and other food safety systems for processors, producers, and food service operators. Includes resources for home-based food preparation businesses.
supporting.users: Food processors, producers selling direct to consumers

economic_impact.note: Food safety training prevents foodborne illness and protects consumer confidence in Alberta products

supporting.name: Comply with meat and dairy inspection requirements
supporting.desc: Provincial inspection of meat processing plants and dairy facilities to ensure safety standards are met. Covers facility licensing, operational standards, and animal welfare at slaughter.
supporting.users: Meat processors, dairy processors, abattoir operators

economic_impact.note: Inspection protects public health and enables domestic and international market access for Alberta products

main_services.name: Manage pests, diseases, and weeds on your land

supporting.name: Report or get help with an agricultural pest
supporting.desc: Designated pests including insects, rodents, and plant diseases must be reported. Agricultural fieldmen and service boards assist with identification, control measures, and compliance.

economic_impact.note: Effective pest control prevents crop losses worth hundreds of millions annually

supporting.name: Control noxious and prohibited weeds
supporting.desc: Landowners are required to control noxious weeds and destroy prohibited noxious weeds on their land. Agricultural fieldmen enforce compliance and provide identification and control guidance.

economic_impact.note: Invasive weeds reduce crop yields and degrade rangeland carrying capacity

supporting.name: Get crop disease diagnosis and management advice
supporting.desc: Information on regulated and common crop diseases, diagnostic services, and integrated pest management strategies. Includes economic threshold data for insect treatment decisions.

economic_impact.note: Accurate diagnosis and targeted treatment reduce pesticide costs and crop losses

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Alberta's agri-food sector grows and competes globally
goals.description: Producers and agribusinesses have the information, infrastructure, and market access to grow Alberta's agriculture and food sector domestically and internationally.

foundational_services.name: Develop agricultural policy, trade strategy, and irrigation infrastructure
foundational_services.desc: Set policy direction for agricultural growth, negotiate trade agreements, administer the Sustainable Canadian Agricultural Partnership (Sustainable CAP), and oversee irrigation district governance and water allocation.
foundational_services.users: Agricultural industry, irrigation districts, rural communities

main_services.name: Access agronomic information and decision tools

supporting.name: Use weather and climate data for farm planning
supporting.desc: The Alberta Climate Information Service (ACIS) provides current and historical weather data, forecasts, growing degree days, moisture indices, and other agricultural climate tools for production planning.

economic_impact.note: Data-driven planting and harvest decisions improve yields and reduce losses

supporting.name: Get soil survey data and land information
supporting.desc: Access soil survey reports, the Alberta Soil Information Viewer, land capability assessments, and nutrient management planning resources to make informed decisions about cropping and land use.

economic_impact.note: Soil-appropriate cropping and nutrient management improve yields and protect long-term productivity

supporting.name: Use crop budget and decision tools
supporting.desc: Online crop budget calculator, cropping alternatives cost-return forecasts, and AgriProfit$ benchmarking data to support production and financial planning decisions.

economic_impact.note: Better financial planning improves farm profitability and reduces risk of loss

main_services.name: Develop markets and grow agricultural trade

supporting.name: Access market information and price reports
supporting.desc: Weekly livestock and crop market reviews, Alberta crop reports, agricultural statistics, and economic indicators to support marketing decisions and industry planning.

economic_impact.note: Timely market information helps producers sell at better prices

supporting.name: Get support for agricultural trade and export
supporting.desc: Services to help agribusinesses access international markets, connect with buyers and investors, and navigate export requirements. Includes trade missions and matchmaking services.

economic_impact.note: Alberta's agri-food exports exceed $17 billion annually; trade services help grow that

supporting.name: Apply for agricultural sustainability funding
supporting.desc: The Sustainable Canadian Agricultural Partnership provides cost-shared grants for eligible projects in environmental sustainability, market development, science and research, and public trust. Five-year federal-provincial-territorial framework.

economic_impact.note: Cost-shared investment accelerates adoption of beneficial management practices and innovation

main_services.name: Access irrigation and water management services

supporting.name: Connect with your irrigation district
supporting.desc: Alberta's 13 irrigation districts manage water delivery infrastructure serving over 1.5 million acres of irrigated farmland. Producers within a district access water allocation, infrastructure expansion, and water-sharing arrangements.

economic_impact.note: Irrigation is the single largest driver of agricultural productivity in southern Alberta

supporting.name: Get farm water testing and quality information
supporting.desc: Testing services for farm drinking water, livestock water, and irrigation water quality. Helps producers identify contamination risks and take corrective action.

economic_impact.note: Safe water protects human and animal health in rural communities

cross_cutting: Office of the Chief Provincial Veterinarian

mandate_legend.label: Ensure food safety and animal health
